badge.artifact_tip: {n} anomalies detected (e.g. door opened mid-cycle) – open to see them on the graph
badge.fair_fit_tip: Moderate match consistency – some cycles assigned to this profile have lower confidence scores. Label more cycles or re-record the profile to improve accuracy.
badge.poor_fit_tip: Inconsistent match history – consider rebuilding this profile
badge.restart_gap_tip: {n} HA restart gap: power trace has a hole
badge.imported_tip: Imported from the community store. Used for matching only, not counted in stats.

btn.auto_label_cycles_tip: Automatically assign profile names to unlabelled cycles whose match confidence clears the threshold
btn.delete_group_tip: Delete this group only - the member profiles are kept
btn.discard_tip: Discard the recorded trace without saving
btn.force_stop_tip: Immediately end the current cycle and mark it as force-stopped
btn.label: Label
btn.new_group_tip: Group near-identical profiles (same shape/duration, different temperature or spin) so the matcher reliably picks between them
btn.new_profile_tip: Create a new program profile from an existing labelled cycle or recording
btn.pause_cycle_tip: Pause the running cycle – the appliance will resume where it left off
btn.process_tip: Save the recorded trace as a new or existing profile
btn.rebuild_tip: Recompute the expected power envelope (min/max band) for all profiles from their labelled cycles – run after labelling new cycles or correcting old ones
btn.rec_start_tip: Begin recording the appliance's power trace – start just before running a cycle
btn.rec_stop_tip: Stop recording and hold the captured trace for review
btn.reset_ml_models_tip: Discard the fine-tuned models and go back to the built-in ones. WashData can re-learn them later.
btn.revert_to_previous: Restore this setting to its previous value
btn.revert_settings_tip: Restore settings from before your last save
btn.run_analysis_tip: Analyze your recorded cycles now and refresh tuning suggestions
btn.pg_apply_to_settings_tip: Copy the values you edited here into this device's live settings
btn.share_device_tip: Share this appliance and its recorded reference cycles to the community store so others with the same machine can adopt them

anti_wrinkle_exit.exit: Must be below Stop Threshold ({stop} W); otherwise the anti-wrinkle exit power is ignored
anti_wrinkle_exit.stop: Must be above Anti-Wrinkle Exit Power ({exit} W)

anti_wrinkle_max.max: Must be above Start Threshold ({start} W); otherwise anti-wrinkle duration limit is bypassed
anti_wrinkle_max.start: Must be below Anti-Wrinkle Max Power ({max} W)

conflict.settings_banner: {n} setting conflict{s} – check the highlighted sections and fix before saving.

confidence.auto: Must be at or above Match Threshold ({match})
confidence.learning: Must be at or below Match Threshold ({match})
confidence.match_for_auto: Must be at or below Auto-Label Confidence ({alc})
confidence.match_for_learning: Must be at or above Learning Confidence ({lc})

duration_ratio.max: Must be greater than Min Duration Ratio ({min})
duration_ratio.min: Must be less than Max Duration Ratio ({max})

hysteresis.start: Must be above Stop Threshold ({stop} W)
hysteresis.stop: Must be below Start Threshold ({start} W)

min_power.min_power: Must be at or below Stop Threshold ({stop} W)
min_power.stop: Must be at or above Min Power ({min} W)

no_update_timeout.timeout: Must be greater than Watchdog Interval ({wi} s)
no_update_timeout.watchdog: Must be less than No-Update Timeout ({to} s)

off_delay.gap: Min Off Gap should be at least Off Delay ({delay} s)
off_delay.off_delay: Off Delay ({off_delay} s) exceeds Min Off Gap ({gap} s); consecutive cycles may merge

power_off.stop: Must be above Power Off Threshold ({pot} W)
power_off.threshold: Must be below Stop Threshold ({stop} W) to take effect

pump_stuck.duration: Must be less than No-Update Timeout ({to} s) so the stuck alarm fires before the watchdog kills the cycle
pump_stuck.timeout: Must exceed Pump Stuck Duration ({ps} s) so the stuck alarm fires before the cycle is force-stopped

start_dur.sampling: Sampling Interval exceeds Start Duration ({sdt} s); single-sample spikes can open a cycle
start_dur.threshold: Should be at least one Sampling Interval ({si} s) to prevent single-sample false starts

unmatch.unmatch: Must be below Match Threshold ({match}); otherwise a committed match un-matches instantly

conflict.suggestion_resolves: Stage the pending suggestion ({val}) below to fix this

watchdog.interval: Should be at least 2x Sampling Interval ({si} s)
watchdog.sampling: Sampling Interval should be at most half of Watchdog Interval ({wi} s)

hdr.manual_recording_tip: Run a cycle intentionally while WashData records the power trace. Start just before the appliance starts, Stop when it finishes, then Process to save it as a named profile.
hdr.no_automations: No automations reference this device yet.

lbl.aria_playground_chart: Cycle power trace with draggable detection thresholds
lbl.default_access_level: Default level for users not listed below
lbl.default_tab: Default tab when opening the panel
lbl.label: Matched program
lbl.pg_tile_ambiguous_tip: Cycles with a near-tie between two programs
lbl.pg_tile_detected_tip: Cycles where power crossed the start threshold long enough to register as running
lbl.pg_tile_matched_tip: Cycles correctly attributed to a known program
lbl.program_tip: Override which profile is matched to the current cycle. Auto-detect lets the integration pick the best match automatically. Pin a specific program to force-match it when auto-detect is wrong or you know what is running.
lbl.show_debug: Show live match debug card on the Status page (confidence, ambiguity, top candidates)
lbl.show_expected: Show expected curve overlay (matched profile, orange)
lbl.show_raw: Show raw socket toggle in live power graph
lbl.zoom_hint: scroll to zoom · dblclick to reset
lbl.aria_playground_chart2: Interactive cycle power graph: hover to read time/power, scroll to zoom, drag to pan
lbl.pg_envelope_fit_tip: How closely the cycle sits inside this profile's saved power envelope - a different lens than match confidence.

ml.trend_improving_tip: This model's fit has improved across recent re-checks.
ml.trend_declining_tip: This model's fit has slipped across recent re-checks – reviewing more cycles may help it re-learn.
ml.trend_steady_tip: This model's fit has held roughly steady across recent re-checks.

cap_blurb.end: Knowing when a cycle has truly finished

modal.delete_maintenance_msg: Delete this maintenance record? This cannot be undone.
modal.process_history_msg: Re-run matching, refresh suggestions, retrain ML (if enabled) and recompute cycle health across all stored cycles. This may take a while.
modal.delete_automation_msg: Delete the automation "{name}" from Home Assistant? This cannot be undone.
modal.remove_legacy_msg: Remove the legacy custom actions? They will stop firing on cycle events. This cannot be undone from the panel.
modal.discard_recording_msg: Discard the saved recording? This cannot be undone.
modal.wipe_all_msg: ⚠️ This permanently deletes ALL cycles and profiles. This cannot be undone.
modal.force_stop_msg: Force-stop the active cycle now? The cycle will be saved as interrupted.

msg.artifact_dip_detail: Dropped below the usual power band for ~{n}s.
msg.artifact_footer: Highlighted on the graph above. These are transient artifacts (e.g. the door opened mid-cycle), not necessarily problems.
msg.artifact_pause_detail: Power dropped to near zero for ~{n}s then resumed – likely the door was opened mid-cycle or the cycle was paused.
msg.artifact_spike_detail: Drew above the usual power band for ~{n}s.
msg.auto_label_intro: Assign profiles to unlabelled cycles whose match confidence clears the threshold.
msg.automations_intro: WashData fires {start} / {end} events and exposes entities, so notifications and actions are best built as normal Home Assistant automations. Automations that use this device appear below.
msg.cleanup_intro: Every labelled cycle overlaid. Tick outliers and delete to clean up the profile.
msg.clear_debug_hint: Remove stored debug data to free space.
msg.collecting_data: Collecting data – {need} more cycle{plural} before fine-tuning can start ({current}/{min}).
msg.compare_profiles_tip: Overlay other profile envelopes on the chart above to see which one best fits this cycle.
msg.coverage_gap: recent cycles have no matching profile ({pct}% of last 30).
msg.coverage_gap_similar_cycles: {count} similar unlabelled cycles found – create a profile to start matching them.
msg.enough_data: Enough data to learn from ({current}/{min} cycles).
msg.export_description: Export all profiles and cycles to JSON, or restore from a previous export.
msg.feedback_prompt: Confirm it was right, correct the program, or ignore.
msg.group_modal_help: Group programs with the same shape that differ in temperature/spin (durations may vary). Matching scores the group as one candidate, then picks the best-fitting member. Pick at least 2; the overlay shows how alike they are.
msg.group_not_cohesive: These profiles aren't similar enough to group reliably, so matching treats them individually until you remove the outlier or split the group.
msg.group_preview_hint: Tick 2+ members to preview and compare their power curves.
msg.import_intro: Load an exported file or paste a JSON payload below.
msg.live_chart_loading: Live power chart appears as readings arrive.
msg.log_buffer_hint: Newest first · buffers the last 500 ha_washdata records since restart · drag the bottom edge to resize.
msg.maintenance_advisory: Increasing duration/energy may indicate appliance maintenance needed (e.g. descaling, filter cleaning).
msg.maintenance_intro: Log servicing you perform on this appliance and get reminded when each task is due again.
msg.maintenance_requires_access: Maintenance and export/import require full access.
msg.manual_duration_hint: The profile's average/expected cycle length, used for time-remaining estimates. Edit to set it; leaving it unchanged keeps the current value.
msg.matching_tuning_intro: When learning, WashData also adjusts how much program matching weighs shape versus duration and energy.
msg.merge_intro: The selected cycles are combined into one (chronological order; gaps filled with 0 W). Pick the resulting profile.
msg.ml_intro: WashData ships with smart models that work out of the box.
msg.ml_settings_intro: Two independent switches: one applies the models while a cycle runs, the other lets WashData fine-tune them to your machine over time.
msg.name_first_program: You have enough cycles – name your first program to start matching.
msg.near_duplicate_cluster: near-duplicate profile cluster detected. Grouping lets matching reliably pick between look-alikes (e.g. same program at different temperature/spin).
msg.no_cycles_match: No cycles match the current filter.
msg.no_cycles_selected: Select at least one cycle first.
msg.imported_readonly: Imported from the community store. Shown for reference and matching. It is not counted in your stats and cannot be edited.
msg.merge_no_imports: Imported recordings cannot be merged into a real cycle. Deselect them to merge.
msg.no_envelope: No envelope yet - rebuild after labelling cycles.
msg.no_fine_tuned: Nothing fine-tuned yet – WashData is using its built-in models.
msg.no_match_yet: No match attempt yet - this populates during a running cycle.
msg.no_other_users: No other Home Assistant users found.
msg.no_profiles_yet: No profiles yet. Create one from a labelled cycle.
msg.notify_services_hint: Use {entity} service IDs (comma-separated for multiple). Template variables: {vars}.
msg.old_actions_warning: Configured with the old actions editor (now removed). They still fire on cycle events but can no longer be edited here. Convert them into a normal automation, or remove them.
msg.onboarding_watching: Run your appliance normally – WashData is watching. After 3 cycles, program matching will begin.
msg.pg_analysis_empty: Load a cycle to see match analysis.
msg.pg_canvas_empty: Select a cycle above, then press Load to see its power trace.
msg.pg_restart_note: Restart Home Assistant to enable simulation tools.
msg.pg_sweep_intro: What if {param} were different? Test {steps} values across your last {cycles} cycles to find the setting where the most cycles are correctly matched.
msg.pg_verdict_bad: Needs attention: many cycles are going undetected.
msg.pg_verdict_good: Well tuned: most cycles are correctly identified and matched.
msg.pg_verdict_ok: Acceptable: some cycles missed. Try lowering the start threshold.
msg.phase_catalog_intro: Named segments of a cycle (Pre-wash, Heating, Spin…). Assign them to a profile from its control panel.
msg.phase_ranges_intro: Phase ranges (minutes from cycle start) overlaid on the average curve. Edit values to preview live.
msg.playground_intro: A safe sandbox to replay past cycles with different settings, compare two setups side by side, and inspect how a cycle aligns to a profile. Nothing here changes your live data until you explicitly apply it.
msg.playground_needs_restart: Restart Home Assistant to enable the Playground tools.
msg.prefs_personal: These apply to your Home Assistant account only.
msg.process_history_hint: Re-run matching on all stored cycles, refresh tuning suggestions, retrain the ML models (if enabled), and recompute cycle health. Run this after a batch of reviews.
msg.profile_poor_health_detail: Cycles assigned to this profile have inconsistent shapes or low confidence. Consider rebuilding the envelope or reviewing labelled cycles.
msg.profiles_intro: Click a profile for stats, phases and cleanup. Group near-identical programs (same shape/duration, different temperature or spin) so matching reliably picks between them.
msg.rbac_hint: When off, every Home Assistant user has full access (the default). Administrators always have full access and can manage everyone.
msg.reminders_intro: Show a reminder in the panel this many cycles after the last service. Leave blank or 0 to turn a reminder off.
msg.restart_gap_footer: Highlighted on the graph. Power data is missing for these intervals; matching used only real readings.
msg.restart_gap_header: {n} HA restart gap during this cycle
msg.review_confirm_help: Confirm whether this cycle was detected correctly. Your reviews train the model on your machine - the more cycles you confirm, the better matching and health scoring get. A quick Good/Bad is enough.
msg.review_notes_tip: Free-text notes for your own reference. Not used by matching or training.
msg.review_profile_tip: The program this cycle is labelled as. If the auto-detected program was wrong, correct it here - labelling teaches matching for future cycles.
msg.review_quality_tip: How clean this cycle is. Good = a textbook example of this program; Bad = detected but noisy or atypical; Unusable = mis-detected (merged, truncated or spurious). Drives the health score and which cycles are allowed to train the model.
msg.review_recorded_tip: Mark this as a hand-picked reference cycle for its program - the same role as a manually recorded cycle. Reference cycles are always kept, seed the matching template, and are never dropped by cleanup. (This is the "golden"/recorded flag; both are the same thing.)
msg.review_tags_tip: Optional flags describing what went wrong with this cycle, so training and cleanup can account for it.
msg.saving_triggers_reload: Saving triggers an integration reload. HA entities may briefly show as unavailable.
msg.settings_basic_note: Showing essential settings. Switch to Advanced for the full list.
msg.shape_drift_detail: The power pattern for this profile has shifted over time – possible appliance wear or maintenance needed (e.g. descaling, filter cleaning).
msg.split_intro: Click the graph to add or remove a split point, or auto-detect by idle gaps. Each resulting segment can get its own profile.
msg.toast_automation_migrated: Actions migrated to an automation; opening editor
msg.toast_convert_rolled_back: Migration failed and was rolled back (no automation left behind): {error}
msg.toast_convert_orphan: The automation was created, but clearing the old actions failed. Do not retry: remove the legacy actions manually to avoid a duplicate automation.
msg.trim_intro: Drag the red handles, or enter values. Everything outside the window is removed.
msg.tuning_suggestions_available: {count} tuning suggestion available from observed cycles. They appear beside the relevant fields.
msg.warmup_detail: This profile needs {needed} labelled cycles before auto-matching begins. Every confirmed cycle helps it learn.
msg.wipe_history_warning: Permanently delete all cycles and profiles. Cannot be undone.
msg.compare_overlay_tip: Overlay learned profile envelopes to see which program each cycle resembles.
msg.pg_select_cycle_hint: Select a cycle and click ↺ to load
msg.pg_analysis_hint: Select a cycle and click ↺ to load analysis.
msg.manual_duration_ref_hint: Only used when no reference cycle is selected – a reference cycle sets the duration from its own length.
msg.advisory_poor_health: '{name}' has a low fit score - its recent cycles vary a lot or match weakly. Review its cycles or re-record the profile so matching and time estimates stay accurate.
msg.advisory_shape_drift: '{name}' has drifted significantly from its original power shape. The appliance may have changed behaviour over time (e.g. limescale, wear). Consider re-recording this profile with recent cycles.
msg.advisory_shape_drift_corr: '{name}' has drifted significantly from its original power shape (correlation {corr}). The appliance may have changed behaviour over time (e.g. limescale, wear). Consider re-recording this profile with recent cycles.
msg.advisory_duration_trend_up: '{name}' cycles are running progressively longer (about +{pct}% per cycle). If the appliance's behaviour changed, re-record or rebuild this profile.
msg.advisory_energy_trend_up: '{name}' is drawing progressively more energy (about +{pct}% per cycle) - worth checking the appliance if that is unexpected.
msg.advisory_phase_inconsistent: '{name}' looks like it mixes different programs or temperatures - its cycles heat for very different lengths of time. Splitting it into separate profiles (e.g. per temperature) will improve matching and time estimates.
msg.pg_row_load_hint: Load this cycle in the graph above
msg.pg_history_intro2: Replay your recent cycles through the real detector and matcher with the settings above. Click any row to load that cycle in the graph; edit a setting to see a before/after comparison.
msg.pg_canvas_empty2: Pick a cycle above and press Run to simulate it. Then hover to read values, scroll to zoom, and drag to pan.
msg.pg_apply_settings_confirm: Save these {n} setting(s) to this device? {list}
msg.no_logs_match: No log records match these filters.
msg.pg_analysis_hint2: Pick a cycle and press Run to load match analysis.
msg.pg_analysis_empty2: Press Run to see match analysis.
msg.store_enable_hint: Enable online features in Settings to browse and import community reference cycles.
msg.online_intro: Share and download reference cycles with other WashData users. All online features are opt-in and off by default.
msg.store_share_intro: Upload this reference cycle so others with the same appliance can use it. It is reviewed before appearing publicly.
msg.online_intro_global: Browse and share reference recordings with other WashData users, and confirm appliance entries. One connection applies to your whole WashData integration; appliance brand and model are set per device under Basic. All online features are opt-in and off by default.
msg.online_unavailable: Online features are not available on this server.
msg.store_picker_offline: Enable online features in the settings gear to pick from the community catalog.
msg.connect_to_confirm: Connect in the settings gear to confirm or rate.
msg.store_share_device_intro: Upload {brand} {model} with the reference cycles you select. Others with the same appliance can adopt your programs. Entries are reviewed before appearing publicly.
msg.share_device_none: No shareable cycles yet. Mark a recorded or hand-picked cycle as a reference cycle (⭐) in the Cycles tab first.
msg.store_download_device_intro: Adopt every shared program and its reference cycles onto your device. Your own recorded cycles and stats are not affected.
msg.onboard_download: New device? Adopt a ready-made setup (programs, reference cycles and phases) from another WashData user with the same appliance.
msg.include_settings_hint: Share this device's recognition and matching thresholds (not your notifications, entities or energy price). Adopters choose whether to apply them.
msg.adopt_settings_hint: Overwrite this device's detection & matching thresholds with the shared ones. Your notifications, entities and energy price are never changed.
msg.share_guideline_naming: Name each profile exactly as shown on the appliance dial or display (e.g. 'Cotton 40', 'Eco 60').
msg.share_guideline_quality: Only share cycles that completed normally -- no mid-cycle interruptions, door-open events, or power blips.
msg.share_guideline_review: Your upload starts as pending and appears publicly once enough community members confirm it.
msg.share_consent: I confirm these cycles ran to normal completion without interruption
msg.share_profile_no_cycles: No reference cycles – mark a cycle as ⭐ in the Cycles tab to include this profile

pg_desc.abrupt_drop_watts: Sudden drop treated as immediate end
pg_desc.completion_min_seconds: Shortest run that counts as a real cycle
pg_desc.end_repeat_count: Low readings in a row before ending
pg_desc.min_off_gap: Gap required to separate two cycles
pg_desc.off_delay: Seconds of low power before cycle ends
pg_desc.start_duration_threshold: Seconds above threshold to confirm start
pg_desc.start_threshold_w: Minimum watts to count as started
pg_desc.stop_threshold_w: Below this, machine counts as off
pg_desc.dtw_bandwidth: Stage 3: Sakoe-Chiba warp band (0 = DTW off; default 0.2 = 20% of cycle length)
pg_desc.corr_weight: Stage 2: balance between curve shape (correlation) and power level (MAE); default 0.45
pg_desc.duration_weight: Stage 4: how strongly run-length agreement affects the final score (default 0.22)
pg_desc.energy_weight: Stage 4: how strongly energy agreement affects the final score (default 0.22)
pg_desc.profile_match_min_duration_ratio: Stage 1: minimum cycle length as a fraction of profile duration; default 0.1 (10%)
pg_desc.profile_match_max_duration_ratio: Stage 1: maximum cycle length as a fraction of profile duration; default 1.5 (150%)
pg_desc.keep_min_score: Stage 2: floor score to stay in the race; default 0.1 admits weak matches, later stages pick the best
pg_desc.dtw_blend: Stage 3: 0 = core score only, 1 = DTW score only, 0.5 = equal blend (default)
pg_desc.dtw_ensemble_w: Stage 3 (ensemble mode): weight on scaled-L1 vs derivative DTW; default 0.7 favours level-aware
pg_desc.dtw_ddtw_scale: Stage 3: DDTW half-saturation distance; smaller = more shape-sensitive (default 30)
pg_desc.dtw_refine_top_n: Stage 3: candidates DTW re-scores; raise to 7-9 if correct profile ranks 4th-5th (default 5)
pg_desc.duration_scale: Stage 4: log-ratio where duration agreement halves; smaller = stricter penalty (default 0.175)
pg_desc.energy_scale: Stage 4: log-ratio where energy agreement halves; smaller = stricter penalty (default 0.25)

phase_desc.anti_crease: Occasional short tumbles after completion to reduce wrinkles.
phase_desc.anti_wrinkle: Periodic post-cycle tumbling to reduce wrinkles.
phase_desc.cool_down: Tumbling without heat near cycle end.
phase_desc.drain_and_switch: Transition period from washing to drying mode.
phase_desc.dry: Drying stage using heater and/or residual heat.
phase_desc.heat_up: Initial heater warm-up before full drying begins.
phase_desc.pre_wash: Initial soak or pre-treatment before the main wash.
phase_desc.rinse: Clean-water rinse stage. This phase may repeat multiple times.
phase_desc.sanitize: High-temperature cleaning stage for sanitization programs.
phase_desc.sensor_check: Short low-power pause while dryness is measured.
phase_desc.soak: Low-activity soaking period between active wash stages.
phase_desc.soak_dw: Extended soak period for heavy soil.
phase_desc.spin: High-speed extraction to remove water from the load.
phase_desc.wash: Main washing cycle with drum movement and optional heating.

placeholder.maintenance_notes: e.g. replaced filter, cleaned door seal

anti_wrinkle.intro: Anti-wrinkle / anti-crease mode detects low-power tumble pulses after the main phase and keeps them attached to the finished cycle.
anti_wrinkle.label: Anti-Wrinkle

basic.intro: Core identity and the essentials most setups need.
basic.label: Basic

delay.intro: Delayed-start detection identifies when an appliance is powered but has not yet begun its cycle.
delay.label: Delayed Start

detection.intro: How a cycle is detected as starting, running and finishing.
detection.label: Detection

matching.intro: How finished cycles are matched to learned profiles and labelled.
matching.label: Matching

ml_training.intro: On-device machine learning retraining schedule and data settings.
ml_training.label: ML Training

notifications.intro: When and how you are notified about cycle events.
notifications.label: Notifications

timing.intro: Background cadence, the offline watchdog and housekeeping.
timing.label: Timing & Watchdog

triggers.intro: Optional external signals: an end trigger, a door sensor, a pause switch, and the unload reminder.
triggers.label: Triggers & Doors

phase_eta.intro: Phase-aware time-remaining, for machines whose cycle length depends on temperature or spin.
phase_eta.label: Time Remaining

abrupt_drop_ratio.doc: A drop larger than this fraction of current power is also treated as abrupt (0.6 = a 60% drop). Complements the watts threshold across appliance sizes.
abrupt_drop_ratio.label: Abrupt Drop Ratio

abrupt_drop_watts.doc: A power drop larger than this flags the cycle as Interrupted (manual cancel) rather than a natural finish.
abrupt_drop_watts.label: Abrupt Drop

anti_wrinkle_enabled.doc: Recognise the short low-power tumble pulses a dryer emits after the main heat phase and keep them attached to the finished cycle instead of reading them as new cycles.
anti_wrinkle_enabled.label: Enable Anti-Wrinkle Detection

anti_wrinkle_exit_power.doc: Power must fall below this between pulses for anti-wrinkle mode to stay active.
anti_wrinkle_exit_power.label: Exit Power Threshold

anti_wrinkle_max_duration.doc: Pulses longer than this are treated as a real cycle rather than an anti-wrinkle tumble.
anti_wrinkle_max_duration.label: Max Duration

anti_wrinkle_max_power.doc: A pulse above this power is treated as a real new cycle, not an anti-wrinkle tumble. Set just above the tumble-pulse power.
anti_wrinkle_max_power.label: Max Anti-Wrinkle Power

auto_label_confidence.doc: If the match score at cycle end is at or above this, the program is labeled automatically without any confirmation prompt. Raise it to require higher certainty before auto-labeling; lower it to automate more. Works in conjunction with Learning Confidence below it.
auto_label_confidence.label: Auto-Label Confidence

auto_maintenance.doc: Run nightly housekeeping: rebuild profile envelopes, recompute cycle health, prune debug traces and retain the most recent cycles.
auto_maintenance.label: Auto Maintenance (nightly cleanup)

completion_min_seconds.doc: Cycles shorter than this are discarded as ghost cycles (test runs, opening the door to add a sock).
completion_min_seconds.label: Min Cycle Duration

delay_confirm_seconds.doc: Power must stay in the standby band for this long before the appliance is treated as waiting-to-start rather than running.
delay_confirm_seconds.label: Confirm Seconds

delay_start_detect_enabled.doc: Detect when the appliance is powered on and waiting (delayed start / standby) but has not begun its cycle, so standby draw is not mistaken for a running cycle.
delay_start_detect_enabled.label: Enable Delay-Start Detection

delay_timeout_hours.doc: Stop waiting in delayed-start mode after this many hours and return to idle, so a machine left powered but never started does not wait forever.
delay_timeout_hours.label: Timeout Hours

device_type.doc: Appliance class. Sets sensible detection defaults (thresholds, off-delay, end handling) tuned for that appliance type; change it only if the device was originally set up as the wrong type.
device_type.label: Device Type

door_sensor_entity.doc: Optional door binary sensor. Used to detect when the appliance has been opened/unloaded after a cycle.
door_sensor_entity.label: Door Sensor Entity

duration_tolerance.doc: Tolerance for time-remaining estimates (learning feedback, not matching). If the actual duration is within +/-X% of the estimate it counts as a good match.
duration_tolerance.label: Estimate Tolerance

enable_ml_models.doc: While a cycle runs, let the models refine the live results: a steadier time-remaining and energy/cost estimate, and an anti-premature-stop guard on end detection (it can only ever delay a finish, never end one early, and is bounded). Uses your fine-tuned models when available, otherwise the built-in ones. Off = the classic power-based logic only (still reliable).
enable_ml_models.label: Apply smart models during a cycle

end_energy_threshold.doc: During the off-delay countdown, accumulated energy (watts x time) is compared to this threshold. If exceeded, the countdown resets - keeping anti-crease tumbles and dishwasher drying tails attached to the cycle instead of cutting them short. Raise it if cycles end too early during cool-down; lower it if detection is sluggish.
end_energy_threshold.label: End Energy

end_repeat_count.doc: Number of consecutive below-stop-threshold readings required before the cycle ends. 1 is fine for most plugs. Raise to 2-3 if your smart plug occasionally reports a false-zero sample mid-cycle and your cycles are ending prematurely.
end_repeat_count.label: End Repeat Count

energy_price_entity.doc: Sensor with the current electricity price per kWh (e.g. a dynamic tariff). Takes precedence over the static price below. Each cycle freezes the price in effect when it finished.
energy_price_entity.label: Energy Price Entity

energy_price_static.doc: Fixed price per kWh used for cost figures when no live price entity is set above.
energy_price_static.label: Static Energy Price (per kWh)

energy_sensor.doc: Optional cumulative energy counter (total_increasing kWh/Wh, e.g. the plug's own lifetime meter). When set, each cycle's reported energy is taken from this counter's start-to-end delta, which avoids the under-counting you get from integrating a slow-reporting power sensor. Falls back to the integrated value if the reading is missing, its unit is unknown, or the delta is not positive. Leave blank to keep integrating the power sensor.
energy_sensor.label: Energy Meter Entity

expose_debug_entities.doc: Publish extra diagnostic HA entities (match confidence, ambiguity, state internals). Off keeps the entity list clean for normal use.
expose_debug_entities.label: Expose Debug Entities

external_end_trigger.doc: Binary sensor whose state change marks the cycle end (e.g. an appliance "finished" contact or a companion integration).
external_end_trigger.label: External Trigger Entity

external_end_trigger_enabled.doc: Let an external binary sensor signal the end of a cycle, in addition to the built-in power-based detection.
external_end_trigger_enabled.label: Enable External End Trigger

external_end_trigger_inverted.doc: Treat the trigger sensor turning OFF (rather than ON) as the end-of-cycle signal.
external_end_trigger_inverted.label: Invert External Trigger (trigger on OFF)

learning_confidence.doc: If the match score falls between this and Auto-Label Confidence, a feedback notification asks you to verify the identified program. Below this score the match is too uncertain to surface. Must be kept below Auto-Label Confidence.
learning_confidence.label: Learning Confidence

linked_device.doc: Optionally nest this WashData device under another device (e.g. the smart plug) in the HA device registry, shown as "Connected via ...".
linked_device.label: Group Under Device

min_off_gap.doc: If the machine powers off for less than this time, the on/off/on sequence is treated as one continuous cycle. Prevents soak programs (machine powers off for several minutes mid-wash) from being split into two separate cycles. Set it shorter than the gap between your back-to-back loads if you want those counted as separate cycles. Device-type defaults protect the typical intra-cycle pause for each appliance.
min_off_gap.label: Min Off Gap

min_power.doc: Absolute minimum power considered active. Readings below this are treated as 0 W (standby), filtering out the phantom load of smart plugs and standby LEDs.
min_power.label: Minimum Power

ml_training_enabled.doc: Periodically study your reviewed cycles overnight and fine-tune the models to this specific machine. A change is only kept when it genuinely scores better on held-out cycles, so this can only help or stay the same - never regress.
ml_training_enabled.label: Learn from this machine

ml_training_hour.doc: Local hour of day (0-23) to do the overnight fine-tuning. Pick a quiet hour such as 2 (02:00).
ml_training_hour.label: Learn at hour

ml_training_interval_days.doc: Re-check for improvements at most once per this many days.
ml_training_interval_days.label: Check at most every

ml_training_min_cycles.doc: Wait until at least this many cycles have been recorded before fine-tuning, so there is enough to learn from.
ml_training_min_cycles.label: Cycles needed first

name.doc: Display name shown in the HA integrations list and device registry.
name.label: Device Name

no_update_active_timeout.doc: If no power updates arrive for this long while running, assume the plug dropped offline and force-stop to avoid a zombie cycle. Default 600 s allows for cloud or mesh lag.
no_update_active_timeout.label: No-Update Timeout

notify_before_end_minutes.doc: Send an Almost Done alert when estimated time remaining drops below this. 0 disables it.
notify_before_end_minutes.label: Pre-End Alert

notify_channel.doc: Android notification channel name for start/live messages (controls per-channel sound and priority on the mobile app). Blank uses the companion app default.
notify_channel.label: Android Channel (start/live)

notify_cycle_timers.doc: Notifications at specific minutes into a cycle (e.g. to add softener). Message supports {device}, {program}, {minutes}. Enable Auto-pause to pause at that point and receive an interactive notification with a Resume button; resume via the panel, the pause/resume service, or the notification action.
notify_cycle_timers.label: Cycle Timers

notify_finish_channel.doc: Android notification channel name for the finish message. Blank reuses the start/live channel.
notify_finish_channel.label: Android Channel (finish)

notify_finish_message.doc: Body sent when a cycle finishes. Template variables: {device}, {duration}, {minutes}, {program}, {energy_kwh}, {cost}, {time_finished}, {vs_typical}, {cycle_count}. {time_finished} and {vs_typical} are most useful here.
notify_finish_message.label: Finish Message

notify_finish_services.doc: notify.* services called when a cycle finishes. Add one per target; leave empty for no finish notification.
notify_finish_services.label: Finish Services

notify_fire_events.doc: Also fire ha_washdata_* events on cycle start/finish so you can build your own automations.
notify_fire_events.label: Fire HA Events for Notifications

notify_icon.doc: Optional mdi icon for the notification (e.g. mdi:washing-machine). Leave blank for the platform default.
notify_icon.label: Notification Icon

notify_live_chronometer.doc: Show a live-updating countdown timer in the notification (on platforms that support it) instead of a static estimate.
notify_live_chronometer.label: Use Live Chronometer

notify_live_interval_seconds.doc: How often live-progress notifications are refreshed while a cycle runs.
notify_live_interval_seconds.label: Live Update Interval

notify_live_overrun_percent.doc: If a cycle runs past its estimate by more than this percentage, send an overrun alert.
notify_live_overrun_percent.label: Live Overrun % Before Alert

notify_live_services.doc: notify.* services called for live progress updates while a cycle runs. Leave empty to disable live-progress notifications.
notify_live_services.label: Live Progress Services

notify_milestone_message.doc: Message for the milestone notification. Template variables: {device}, {cycle_count}.
notify_milestone_message.label: Milestone Message

notify_milestones.doc: Comma-separated cycle counts that trigger a one-off celebration notification when reached (e.g. 50, 100, 500, 1000). Blank disables milestone notifications.
notify_milestones.label: Cycle Milestones

notify_only_when_home.doc: Only send notifications when at least one of the linked people (above) is home.
notify_only_when_home.label: Notify Only When Home

notify_people.doc: person.* entities used by "Notify Only When Home" to decide whether anyone is home.
notify_people.label: People (for Only When Home)

notify_pre_complete_message.doc: Body of the pre-end / almost-done alert. Template variables: {device}, {duration}, {minutes}, {program}, {energy_kwh}, {cost}, {time_finished}, {vs_typical}, {cycle_count}.
notify_pre_complete_message.label: Pre-Complete Message

notify_quiet_end_hour.doc: End of the do-not-disturb window (0-23). Held notifications are delivered at this hour. Leave blank to disable.
notify_quiet_end_hour.label: Quiet Hours End

notify_quiet_start_hour.doc: Start of a do-not-disturb window (0-23). Finish, reminder and clean-laundry notifications that would fire during quiet hours are held and delivered when the window ends. Leave blank to disable. Supports windows that cross midnight (e.g. start 22, end 7).
notify_quiet_start_hour.label: Quiet Hours Start

notify_reminder_message.doc: Body of the still-waiting unload reminder. Blank uses the built-in default. Template variables: {device}, {duration}, {minutes}, {program}, {energy_kwh}, {cost}, {time_finished}, {vs_typical}, {cycle_count}.
notify_reminder_message.label: Reminder Message

notify_start_message.doc: Body sent when a cycle starts. Template variables: {device}, {duration}, {minutes}, {program}, {energy_kwh}, {cost}, {time_finished}, {vs_typical}, {cycle_count}.
notify_start_message.label: Start Message

notify_start_services.doc: notify.* services called when a cycle starts. Add one per target (phone, dashboard, etc.); leave empty for no start notification.
notify_start_services.label: Start Services

notify_timeout_seconds.doc: Automatically dismiss the notification after this many seconds (on platforms that support it). 0 keeps it until dismissed manually.
notify_timeout_seconds.label: Auto-Dismiss After

notify_unload_delay_minutes.doc: Minutes after a cycle ends before sending the still-waiting "unload the machine" reminder. Set 0 to disable the reminder.
notify_unload_delay_minutes.label: Unload Nag Delay

off_delay.doc: Time to wait after power drops before declaring the cycle finished. If power resumes within this window the cycle continues seamlessly - this bridges pauses between wash stages. Dishwashers have long drying phases (power off for 20-60 min) so the off-delay must exceed that to keep the whole wash+dry as one cycle.
off_delay.label: Off Delay

pause_cuts_power.doc: When a cycle is paused, also switch off the Switch Entity below. Only for appliances whose plug can safely be cut mid-cycle.
pause_cuts_power.label: Pause Also Cuts Power (via switch)

peak_rate_message.doc: Optional custom text for the peak-rate tip appended to the start notification. Template variables: {device}, {price}. Blank uses the built-in default.
peak_rate_message.label: Peak-Rate Message

peak_rate_threshold.doc: When a cycle starts and the current price per kWh is at or above this value, append a peak-rate tip to the start notification. 0 or blank disables the tip.
peak_rate_threshold.label: Peak-Rate Threshold (per kWh)

power_off_delay.doc: How long power must stay below the Power Off Threshold after a cycle finishes before the state returns to Off. Only used when the Power Off Threshold is above 0. Checked on the background cadence, so the effective delay rounds up to the next state-expiry tick.
power_off_delay.label: Power Off Delay

power_off_threshold_w.doc: Optional power-based Off detection. When above 0, once a cycle has finished and power stays below this level for the Power Off Delay, the machine is treated as switched off and the state returns to Off. Leave at 0 to disable (the default). Set it above the true switched-off floor and below the Stop Threshold and your machine's finished-but-on standby draw; if it is not below the Stop Threshold it is ignored. When enabled it replaces the Progress Reset Delay for returning to Off, so a finished machine stays in Finished/Clean until it is actually powered off.
power_off_threshold_w.label: Power Off Threshold

power_sensor.doc: The sensor entity reporting live power in watts for this appliance (e.g. sensor.washer_power). All cycle detection is based on this signal.
power_sensor.label: Power Sensor

profile_duration_tolerance.doc: The +/- band around a profile average duration used during matching. 0.25 means a 60 min profile matches 45-75 min cycles.
profile_duration_tolerance.label: Profile Duration Tolerance

profile_match_interval.doc: How often to attempt profile matching during a running cycle. Default 300 s (5 minutes) balances detection speed and CPU.
profile_match_interval.label: Match Interval

profile_match_max_duration_ratio.doc: Maximum cycle length relative to the profile. 1.3 means a cycle must be under 130% of the profile duration to match.
profile_match_max_duration_ratio.label: Max Duration Ratio

profile_match_min_duration_ratio.doc: Minimum cycle length relative to the profile. 0.9 means a cycle must be at least 90% of the profile duration to match.
profile_match_min_duration_ratio.label: Min Duration Ratio

profile_match_threshold.doc: Minimum similarity score (0-1) required at cycle end to accept a program identification. Raise it to reduce wrong identifications; lower it if your machine's programs are not being matched. Default 0.4 is a conservative starting point.
profile_match_threshold.label: Match Threshold

profile_unmatch_threshold.doc: If a live mid-cycle match drops below this score, the tentative identification is cleared. Keep it a little below the Match Threshold so a brief dip in similarity does not flip the display back to unmatched.
profile_unmatch_threshold.label: Unmatch Threshold

progress_reset_delay.doc: After finishing, hold progress at 100% for this long so Completed is visible on dashboards before resetting to Idle.
progress_reset_delay.label: Progress Reset Delay

pump_stuck_duration.doc: Seconds a pump may run continuously before it is flagged as possibly stuck (fires the stuck-pump event).
pump_stuck_duration.label: Pump Stuck Duration

running_dead_zone.doc: After a cycle starts, power dips within this window are ignored. Washing machines fill with cold water (dropping near 0 W before heating) - without this protection that fill phase looks like a cycle end. This does NOT skip data: the full power trace is recorded from T=0. The suggestion engine measures your machine's actual startup pattern and sizes this automatically.
running_dead_zone.label: Running Dead Zone

sampling_interval.doc: Expected time between sensor readings - used to size the smoothing window and start debounce correctly. Every sensor update is captured regardless of this value; it only calibrates the downstream calculations. The suggestion engine measures your sensor's actual cadence from past cycles and sets this automatically.
sampling_interval.label: Sampling Interval

save_debug_traces.doc: Store the full power trace and matching debug data for each cycle. Useful for troubleshooting but increases storage size.
save_debug_traces.label: Save Debug Traces

smoothing_window.doc: How much the raw power signal is smoothed. Low (2) is responsive but noisy; high (5) smooths spikes but adds lag.
smoothing_window.label: Smoothing Window

start_duration_threshold.doc: Power must stay above the start threshold this long to confirm a real start, preventing split-second on/off toggles from starting a cycle.
start_duration_threshold.label: Start Duration

start_energy_threshold.doc: Energy (power x time) the appliance must consume before RUNNING. A brief high-power spike has very low energy and is ignored, preventing false starts.
start_energy_threshold.label: Start Energy

start_threshold_w.doc: Power must rise above this level to confirm a cycle has started. Setting it too low causes false starts from standby power; too high and slow-starting programs (cold fill) are missed. The suggestion engine sets this just above the machine's observed lowest active power.
start_threshold_w.label: Start Threshold

stop_threshold_w.doc: Power must fall below this level before the off-delay countdown begins. Set it below the Start Threshold - the gap between them is the hysteresis band that prevents flicker. If set too high, low-power phases (rinse holds, anti-crease) falsely trigger the end sequence.
stop_threshold_w.label: Stop Threshold

switch_entity.doc: Optional switch toggled off on pause and back on when resuming, used together with "Pause also cuts power".
switch_entity.label: Switch Entity

watchdog_interval.doc: How often the background watchdog checks for stalled sensors and elapsed timeouts. Default 30 s.
watchdog_interval.label: Watchdog Interval

dtw_bandwidth.label: Shape Match Tolerance
dtw_bandwidth.doc: How much the matcher may time-warp a cycle to fit a saved program's shape. Higher tolerates more speed variation; too high risks matching the wrong program.

corr_weight.label: Shape vs Level Weight
corr_weight.doc: Balance between curve shape and absolute power level when scoring a match. Higher favours shape; lower favours matching the power level.

duration_weight.label: Duration Weight
duration_weight.doc: How strongly a program whose typical run-length matches this cycle is preferred.

energy_weight.label: Energy Weight
energy_weight.doc: How strongly a program whose typical energy use matches this cycle is preferred.

store_brand.label: Appliance Brand
store_brand.doc: Optional. The appliance brand, picked from the community catalog. Used to find and share matching reference recordings. Leave blank if you are not using online features.

store_model.label: Appliance Model
store_model.doc: Optional. The appliance model, picked from the community catalog once a brand is set. If your model is not listed you can add it to the catalog.

show_contributor.doc: Show the "by <contributor>" attribution on community appliances and reference cycles.

enable_phase_matching.doc: Break each running cycle into phases (heating, wash, spin) and budget the time remaining per phase, blended with the classic estimate - leaning on the phase budget early in the cycle and the classic estimate near the end. This personalises the countdown to how long your machine actually heats and runs, which is most noticeable in the first half of a cycle. Off = the classic estimate only. Only the time-remaining display is affected; program matching and cycle detection are unchanged.
enable_phase_matching.label: Phase-aware time remaining

keep_min_score.label: Min Match Score
keep_min_score.doc: Minimum similarity score a candidate needs to stay in the match race. The default 0.1 is deliberately permissive - it admits even weak candidates and relies on later stages to find the best match. Raise it to prune unlikely profiles earlier; lower it to widen the initial candidate pool.

dtw_blend.label: Warp Blend
dtw_blend.doc: How much the time-warp (DTW) alignment score replaces the Stage 2 core score. 0 = use only the Stage 2 score, 1 = use only the DTW score, 0.5 (default) = equal blend. Raise it to rely more on warp alignment when programs have similar power levels but different timing patterns.

dtw_ensemble_w.label: Warp Ensemble Mix
dtw_ensemble_w.doc: In ensemble DTW mode (the default), the weight on scaled L1 versus derivative DTW (DDTW). 1.0 = all scaled-L1, 0 = all DDTW, 0.7 = default. The scaled-L1 variant compares power levels; the derivative variant reacts to how power changes over time. Ensemble blends both.

dtw_ddtw_scale.label: Derivative Warp Scale
dtw_ddtw_scale.doc: Half-saturation distance for derivative DTW scoring. The score halves when the DDTW distance equals this value. Smaller = more sensitive to shape differences. Default 30 is calibrated to typical appliance power traces. Only affects ensemble and ddtw DTW modes.

dtw_refine_top_n.label: Warp Refine Count
dtw_refine_top_n.doc: How many top Stage 2 candidates are re-scored by DTW. DTW is more expensive so it is only applied to the best N candidates. Default 5. Raise it (to 7-9) if the correct profile sometimes only reaches 4th or 5th place after Stage 2 - DTW can rescue it. Lowering speeds up matching slightly.

duration_scale.label: Duration Sharpness
duration_scale.doc: Sharpness of the Stage 4 duration agreement penalty. This is the log-ratio at which the agreement score halves. Smaller = stricter: a duration mismatch hurts more. Default 0.175 corresponds to roughly 18% duration tolerance at half-weight. Pair with Duration Weight.

energy_scale.label: Energy Sharpness
energy_scale.doc: Sharpness of the Stage 4 energy agreement penalty. Smaller = stricter: an energy mismatch hurts more. Default 0.25 is intentionally more forgiving than Duration Sharpness because energy varies with load. Pair with Energy Weight.

auto_label_confidence.higher: Labels fewer cycles automatically; more go to review
auto_label_confidence.lower: Labels more cycles automatically; some may be misidentified

duration_tolerance.higher: Accepts a wider duration range – more flexible matching
duration_tolerance.lower: Requires closer duration match – stricter but may reject unusual cycles

end_energy_threshold.higher: Closes only cycles that used substantial energy
end_energy_threshold.lower: Closes shorter or lower-energy cycles too

end_repeat_count.higher: Requires the end signal to repeat more times – more robust, slightly slower
end_repeat_count.lower: Ends after fewer repetitions – faster detection, more false-end risk

learning_confidence.higher: Learns only from very confident matches – slower but more reliable
learning_confidence.lower: Learns from more cycles; model may be noisier

min_off_gap.higher: Longer idle needed before a new cycle starts
min_off_gap.lower: Brief idle triggers a new cycle – back-to-back runs may split

min_power.higher: Less sensitive to idle power draw; may miss very quiet programs
min_power.lower: More sensitive; may log standby power as activity

no_update_active_timeout.higher: More time before declaring a cycle stuck

off_delay.higher: More time for appliance pauses – handles long soak or drying phases
off_delay.lower: Faster end detection – works well for clean on/off appliances

profile_match_threshold.higher: Commits only high-confidence matches; more cycles stay unlabelled
profile_match_threshold.lower: Matches more cycles; some may be slightly wrong

running_dead_zone.higher: Ignores brief power spikes during idle – less noise-sensitive
running_dead_zone.lower: Responds to shorter power bursts – catches fast transient activity

start_threshold_w.higher: Avoids false starts from brief power spikes
start_threshold_w.lower: Catches low-power programs; may trigger on noise

stop_threshold_w.higher: Ends cycle faster – may close during a pause
stop_threshold_w.lower: Waits longer to end – fewer premature completions

watchdog_interval.higher: Longer quiet phases allowed; fewer false force-stops

reason.watchdog: Kept as low as safe (3x the p95 update gap of {p95}s, min 30s) so stalls are caught quickly without false stops.
reason.off_delay_pauses: Sized to outlast real pauses: p95 intra-cycle pause {p95}s + 60s buffer, from {pauses} pauses across {cycles} clean cycles (floor {floor}s).
reason.tol_per_profile: p75 of per-profile duration variance across {profiles} profiles ({cycles} cycles); tight profiles not penalised.
reason.tol_pooled: Based on pooled duration variance of {cycles} recent labeled cycles (p95 dev={dev}).
reason.min_duration_ratio: Kept as low as possible so a program is recognised early in the cycle; the confidence and ambiguity gates prevent premature commits.
reason.sampling_interval: Median update interval observed across {cycles} clean cycles ({si}s).{excl}
reason.smoothing_window: Sized to smooth ~30s of readings at {si}s sampling ({samples} samples).
reason.start_duration: Kept short (~one {si}s sample interval) so detection starts as early as possible while still ignoring single-sample spikes.
reason.min_power: 40% of the p05 lowest active power ({p05}W) across {cycles} clean cycles, keeping the off-gate below real draw.{excl}
reason.completion_min_seconds: Half the p05 clean-cycle duration ({minutes} min) across {cycles} cycles; filters ghost cycles.{excl}
reason.learning_confidence: p05 confidence of {cycles} user-labeled cycles ({p05}); below this, request verification.
reason.auto_label_confidence: 15th-percentile confidence of {cycles} auto-labels the user never corrected ({p15}).
reason.profile_match_threshold: p10 confidence of {cycles} correct auto-labels ({p10}); safe live-commit floor.
reason.end_repeat_count: {false}/{total} clean cycles ({pct}%) had a >60s internal pause that resumed; require {val} end confirmation(s).
reason.min_off_gap: Based on {gaps} observed inter-cycle gaps (p05={p05}s). Device floor: {floor}s.
reason.min_active: Based on minimum active power ({min}W) observed in last cycle.
reason.end_energy_default: Default recommended baseline for end-of-cycle noise gate.
reason.dead_zone_from_dip: Based on early power dip detected at {s}s.
reason.thr_batch: Kept just above the p05 lowest active power across {cycles} cycles ({p05}W) so a start is caught as early as possible and the stop threshold stays below the machine's lowest running power.
reason.end_energy_false: p95 false-end energy ({p95}Wh) across {cycles} cycles, floored at 0.2% of median cycle energy ({floor}Wh).
reason.end_energy_no_false: No false ends across {cycles} cycles; floored at 0.2% of median cycle energy ({floor}Wh).
reason.dead_zone_batch: Kept short (median startup-instability window across {cycles} cycles = {s}s) so a real end is detected promptly.
reason.ml_off_delay: End-detector-confirmed pauses: p95 {p95}s + 60s buffer, from {pauses} model-verified pauses across {cycles} cycles (floor {floor}s).
reason.ml_end_repeat: {false}/{total} cycles ({pct}%) had a pause the end-detector scored >50%; require {val} end confirmation(s).
reason.ml_auto_label: Lowest confidence the quality model still rated clean (p10 of {cycles} clean cycles = {p10}).

toast.delete_partial_failed: Some items could not be deleted and were restored
toast.min_2_profiles: Select at least 2 profiles for a group
toast.no_split_found: No idle gaps found to split on
toast.settings_conflicts: Fix the highlighted setting conflicts before saving.
toast.saved_except_conflicts: Saved. Fix the highlighted conflicts to save the rest.
toast.pg_run_gone: That run is no longer available.
toast.store_unavailable: The community store is not available.
toast.store_pick_profile: Pick a profile to merge into
toast.store_no_appliance: Set your appliance brand and model in Settings first.
toast.store_shared: Shared to the community store - pending review.
toast.store_device_shared: Shared {n} cycle(s) to the community store - pending review.
toast.share_device_none_sel: Select at least one cycle to share
toast.store_device_shared_partial: Shared {n} cycle(s); {failed} could not be uploaded.
toast.store_download_nothing: Nothing new to download - this setup is already on your device.
toast.store_device_shared_all_dup: All {n} cycle(s) were already in the community store.
toast.store_device_shared_some_dup: Shared {created} cycle(s); {dup} were already in the store.
toast.store_device_downloaded_settings: {p} program(s), {c} recording(s), {ph} phase map(s), {s} setting(s) added

col.profile_tip: Matched program name. Unlabelled means no profile matched at end of cycle.
col.status_tip: Cycle outcome: Completed (natural end), Interrupted (abrupt power drop), Force Stopped (manual), or Needs Review (feedback pending).
col.date_tip: Date and time the cycle started.
col.duration_tip: Total cycle run time from start to end.
col.energy_tip: Total energy consumed (kWh). Computed by integrating power over time.
col.cost_tip: Energy cost for this cycle, frozen at completion using the price in effect then (energy x price per kWh). Set a price under Settings to populate it.
col.confidence_tip: Profile match confidence (0-100%). How closely the cycle power curve matched the identified program.
col.health_tip: ML cycle health (higher = better). Click a cycle to inspect and review it.
col.flags_tip: Review, anomaly and source flags for the cycle. Hover an icon for detail.

pg_evd.detected: The detector recognized a cycle had started.
pg_evd.match_commit: The matcher committed to a program with enough confidence.
pg_evd.match_changed: The leading program changed as more of the cycle was seen.
pg_evd.match_ambiguous: Two programs scored close together, so the match was uncertain.
pg_evd.unmatched: No saved program fit this cycle.
pg_evd.notify_start: A start notification would be sent.
pg_evd.notify_pre_complete: An almost-done notification would be sent.
pg_evd.notify_finish: A finish notification would be sent.
pg_evd.notify_milestone: A milestone notification would be sent.
pg_evd.notify_held: A notification was held back for quiet hours.
pg_evd.finished: The cycle reached a terminal state and ended.

store.no_results: No matching appliances found. Try a different search.
store.no_programs: No shared programs for this appliance yet.
store.no_cycles: No reference cycles shared for this program yet.
store.not_connected: Not connected. Connect a GitHub account to share your own cycles.

tip.add_brand: Brand not listed? Add it to the community catalog
tip.add_appliance: Model not listed? Add your appliance to the community catalog
tip.add_profile: Add a profile for this appliance on the community site

phase0.washer: WashData is already detecting your cycles. Record your first cycle to enable program names and time estimates.
phase0.dishwasher: WashData is watching. Dishwashers have complex cycles – recording your first cycle is strongly recommended. If a detected cycle runs too long, use the cycle editor to trim it before saving as a profile.
phase0.generic: WashData is watching. Record or label a detected cycle to start building profiles.

phase1a.labelled: Good start – your first program is saved. For the cleanest data, consider recording your next cycle with the recorder widget.

phase1b.recorded: Your recording was saved as {profile_name}. Now record or label your other common programs to build coverage.

phase1c.verify: You have {count} programs from the community. Run a cycle to verify WashData recognises it correctly – matching will improve as your device builds its own history.

phase2.cluster: WashData has seen {count} cycles that don't match any saved program – they look similar to each other. Want to create a new profile for them?
phase2.unmatched: Your last cycle didn't match any saved program. Is it a new program?

phase3.suggestions: WashData has settings recommendations based on your cycle history – review them to improve detection accuracy.
phase3.groups: Some of your profiles look like the same program at different temperatures. Organise them into a group for better matching.
phase3.phases: Add program phases to {profile_name} for more accurate time-remaining estimates.

phase4.healthy: This device is fully set up ({profile_count} profiles).

cta.label_detected_cycle: I already have a detected cycle – label it instead
